description_long: This is a small, single-propeller military trainer airplane with a low-wing configuration and a conventional tail. The fuselage is primarily silver-gray with bold red accents on the nose, wingtips, and tail, and it features roundel insignias on the wings and tail, indicating Royal Navy markings. The cockpit is enclosed with a clear canopy and appears to be a single-seat or tandem-seat arrangement. The landing gear is tricycle type, with two main wheels under the wings and a nose wheel, all of which are fixed and visible. The wings are straight with no visible winglets or dihedral, and the propeller is mounted at the nose. The overall design is streamlined for training or light utility roles, with visible panel lines and surface details.
description: A small, low-wing, single-propeller military trainer airplane with red and silver-gray Royal Navy markings, enclosed cockpit, and fixed tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the red nose, propeller, cockpit canopy, and Royal Navy markings on the fuselage and tail.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the propeller, symmetrical wing layout with roundels, and fixed tricycle landing gear.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the red tail, cockpit, and wing roundels, with clear visibility of the landing gear.
description_view_3: Direct rear view showing the tail structure, red accents, and the alignment of the main and nose landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and one nose wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy, likely single or tandem seat.
